notes: Hydroelectric power plant reconstruction. RDNA3 Table A2.1 cites HPP replacement cost benchmarks based on Eastern European unit costs. Range reflects variation in turbine-generator configuration and dam structural complexity. Does not include reservoir reinstatement or downstream ecological remediation (separate cost centre). Source: RDNA3 p.84, cross-checked against EBRD Ukraine Energy Assessment 2023.

Thermal power plant reconstruction (coal or gas-fired). Baseline path assumes like-for-like replacement to existing fuel type. Build-back-better path assumes fuel switch to combined-cycle gas or hybrid, adding 30-60% to unit cost via path_multiplier. RDNA3 §Energy sector reconstruction benchmarks, corroborated by DTEK Energy public communications (2024) on Trypilska and Zmiivska TPP damage assessments.

High-voltage substation (110kV–750kV). Per-MVA reconstruction benchmark from RDNA3 energy sector annex. Range spans simple switching stations (low end) to complex transformer + control-system facilities (high end). DTEK and Ukrenergo public damage assessments 2022–2024 consistent with this range.

Wind farm reconstruction per installed MW. Based on EBRD Ukraine Renewable Energy Framework case studies and IRENA Renewable Power Generation Costs 2023. Range reflects turbine size (2–5 MW units) and transmission infrastructure requirements.

Utility-scale solar PV reconstruction per installed MW. EBRD Ukraine Sustainable Energy Finance Facility benchmarks. Low end: panel replacement on existing frames; high end: complete site reconstruction including grid connection.

Underground or surface gas storage facility reconstruction per bcm (billion cubic metres) of working capacity. RDNA3 energy sector benchmarks, cross-checked against GTS Ukraine technical documentation.

Tertiary or specialist hospital reconstruction per gross floor area m². RDNA3 Table B3.2 healthcare sector benchmarks: USD 5,500–9,000/m² for reconstruction of complex clinical facilities. Build-back-better path adds specialist equipment, energy systems, and digital health infrastructure. KSE healthcare sector damage assessment 2023 consistent with this range for Okhmatdyt class facilities.
Oblast-level general hospital per m². RDNA3 healthcare sector benchmark, consistent with EBRD Social Infrastructure Fund Ukraine case studies.
Raion-level district hospital per m². RDNA3 healthcare sector lower tier. Consistent with Ministry of Health of Ukraine reconstruction cost norms (DBN V.2.2-10).
Maternity and neonatal hospital per m². Higher unit cost than district hospital due to specialist MEP requirements (neonatal life support, sterile environments). RDNA3 healthcare benchmark.
Outpatient polyclinic per m². RDNA3 primary health infrastructure benchmark.

University and higher-education campus building per m². RDNA3 Table B4.1 education sector benchmarks. Range spans standard lecture facilities (low) to specialist laboratory and research buildings (high). KSE education damage assessment 2023 consistent with this range.

State secondary school per m². RDNA3 education sector benchmark. Build-back-better path adds bomb shelter upgrade, energy efficiency envelope, and digital classroom infrastructure. Ministry of Education DBN norms.
Vocational and technical education facility per m². Higher than secondary school due to workshop and laboratory MEP requirements.
Pre-school and kindergarten per m². RDNA3 education lower tier.

Residential district-scale apartment block reconstruction per m² of gross floor area. KSE housing damage assessment methodology 2023. Low end: Soviet Khrushchevka or Brezhnevka panel construction; high end: post-Soviet construction standard or build-back-better. RDNA3 §Housing corroborates range of USD 700–1,500/m² for Ukrainian housing reconstruction. Note: district-scale costing captures external works, utilities, and public space.
Single apartment building per m². Slightly higher than district-scale due to proportionally higher overhead.

Private housing (individual dwellings) per unit. KSE housing assessment; range reflects dwelling size (50–150 m²) and construction standard. eRecovery platform data on Bucha, Irpin, Borodyanka consistent with USD 30,000–55,000 average per dwelling for completed grants.

Performing arts venue (theatre, opera) base unit cost per m² before heritage-conservation premium. Heritage premium of 1.8x–3.0x applied separately on top of base cost (stored in heritage_premium_multiplier fields). Premium reflects authentic material reinstatement, specialist craft trades, conservation architect supervision, and extended programme. RDNA3 cultural-heritage sector annex. ICOMOS Ukraine standards for post-conflict restoration used as calibration benchmark.

Museum and gallery per m², before heritage premium. Higher base than theatre due to collections-environment MEP (HVAC, humidity control, security). RDNA3 cultural sector. Heritage premium 1.8x–3.0x as per conservation standard.

Religious heritage building (church, monastery, cathedral) per m². Wide heritage premium range (2.0x–4.0x) reflecting variation between simple village church (lower) and UNESCO-listed monastic complex (higher). ICOMOS Ukraine; Council of Europe cultural heritage reconstruction programme precedents.
Heritage library and archive per m². RDNA3 cultural sector. Lower heritage premium ceiling than museum/theatre (archival function is partly serviceable in modern construction).
Memorial and monumental structure per m² of covered or paved site. Heritage premium reflects sculptural and material conservation. High-end includes significant original material reinstatement.

Airport reconstruction per m² of terminal and operational building area. RDNA3 transport sector benchmarks. Does not include runway and airside infrastructure (separate cost at USD 3–8M/km per runway). Hostomel calibration: DTEK/MinInfra estimates for comparable aviation infrastructure. Range reflects domestic terminal (low) to international terminal with full airside (high).

Strategic transport aircraft reconstruction per airframe. KSE damage assessment for An-225 Mriya. Low end: rebuild leveraging the ~50–60% complete second An-225 airframe at Kyiv Sviatoshyn facility (Antonov Company estimate). High end: full new-build program. DTEK/Antonov public statements 2022–2024; Ukrainian government An-225 restoration programme feasibility study. Note: this asset_type applies only to unique strategic aircraft; civilian commercial aircraft use insurance replacement value.

Road or railway bridge reconstruction per m² of deck area. RDNA3 transport sector benchmark. Low: standard highway bridge; high: major cable-stay or suspension bridge, or bridge with complex underwater foundation replacement. Antonivskyi Bridge (Kherson) calibration: MinInfra public estimate of USD 300–500M for 1,400m crossing implies USD 21,000–35,000/m² of deck area.

Seaport and river port reconstruction per m² of total operational area (quays, terminals, handling). RDNA3 transport sector. Does not include dredging or maritime access channel works. Calibration: Mariupol Sea Port damage assessment and pre-war asset valuation (USPA Mariupol).

Railway track and associated infrastructure per route-km. RDNA3 transport sector. Low: single-track rural line; central: double-track mainline; high: electrified double-track with signalling upgrade. Ukrzaliznytsia damage assessment 2022–2024; EBRD Railway Modernisation case studies.
Shipyard and ship repair facility per m². RDNA3 industrial sector benchmarks applied to maritime industrial facilities. Includes dry dock, heavy lifting infrastructure, and heavy MEP.

Water supply system (intake, treatment, distribution) per m³/day of rated capacity. RDNA3 municipal infrastructure sector benchmark. KSE water sector damage assessment 2023. Low: rural/small-town distribution; high: large urban system with treatment plant. Mykolaiv/Inhulets supply calibration: OCHA situation reports and Vodokanal damage assessments.

Wastewater treatment plant per m³/day of rated treatment capacity. RDNA3 municipal sector. Higher unit cost than supply due to biological treatment process plant and regulatory compliance requirements.

Irrigation canal and water conveyance infrastructure per km. RDNA3 agriculture sector and water sector. North Crimean Canal calibration: OCHA and Ukrvodgospresuurs damage estimates. Low: earthen unlined canal; high: lined concrete canal with control structures.

District heating pipe network per km (both supply and return). RDNA3 municipal infrastructure. Includes insulated pre-insulated pipe replacement, heat substations, and CHP plant works. Build-back-better path adds heat pump retrofit and smart control systems.

Integrated steelworks reconstruction per tonne/year of annual crude steel capacity. RDNA3 industry sector. KSE industrial damage assessment; Metinvest/Azovstal public documentation 2022–2023. Low: partial brownfield restart; high: complete greenfield equivalent with EAF (electric arc furnace) upgrade for build-back-better. Note: Azovstal and Illich at 7–8 Mtpa implies total reconstruction USD 2.8–7.6B per plant.

Coke oven battery and associated plant per tonne/year of metallurgical coke capacity. RDNA3 industrial sector. Note: long-run viability of coke production is sensitive to steelmaking decarbonisation trajectory; build-back-better path cost is penalised upward by lower path_multiplier attractiveness.
Chemical and fertiliser plant per tonne/year of production capacity. RDNA3 industrial sector. Range is wide due to variation in product complexity (nitrogen fertilisers vs. specialty chemicals).
Heavy machinery or defence industrial facility per m². RDNA3 industrial sector benchmark.

Grain terminal and silo complex per tonne of storage capacity. RDNA3 agriculture sector. Calibration: Odesa and Mykolaiv grain terminal damage assessments by Ukraine Grain Association and USDA attaché reports 2022–2023. Low: simple flat-bottom steel silos; high: vertical reinforced concrete silos with full handling equipment.
Agricultural processing facility per m². RDNA3 agriculture sector.
Regional or oblast administration building per m². RDNA3 public administration sector. Includes fit-out of government operational space, IT infrastructure, and security systems.
Municipal or regional public library (non-heritage) per m². RDNA3 public administration/social infrastructure sector. Build-back-better path adds digital library systems and community learning hub.
Court and justice facility per m². Higher unit cost than standard office due to security, acoustic, and specialist courtroom requirements.
Fire station, police station, or civil defence facility per m². Includes hardened construction, vehicle access, and emergency communications infrastructure.
